headline: Microsoft’s Defense Deal And Clean Energy Push Reframe Long-Term Valuation
summary: Microsoft, ticker NasdaqGS:MSFT, is at the center of a new $9.7b, five year enterprise software consolidation contract with the U.S. Department of Defense. The company has also joined Amazon, Google, and Meta in launching the Data Center Innovation Initiative focused on clean energy and sustainable materials for data centers. These moves touch on U.S. government technology adoption, long term software standard setting, and the environmental profile of AI and cloud infrastructure. Microsoft...

headline: TeamViewer Appoints Peter Ruchatz as Chief Marketing Officer
summary: TeamViewer today announced the appointment of Peter Ruchatz as Chief Marketing Officer (CMO), effective September 2026. Ruchatz is an accomplished B2B software marketing leader with a proven track record of delivering strong growth in Enterprise and SMB segments. He will join TeamViewer's Senior Leadership Team and lead the global marketing organization across brand, product marketing, demand generation, channel marketing, and pricing.

content.title: TeamViewer Appoints Peter Ruchatz as Chief Marketing Officer

headline: From diapers to drugs: How India's global corporate hubs are putting AI to work
summary: Whether they are recruiting the ideal "momfluencer" to sell diapers or trawling through reams of data to fast-track drug launches, global centers of multinational ‌firms in India are increasingly putting AI to work in creative ways. Heads of several Global ‌Capability Centres (GCCs) told Reuters they are deploying AI across a host of functions - from marketing and content creation to finance and human resources - ​to automate time-consuming, repetitive tasks that once required hours of manual effort. And the ambition doesn't stop there.

content.title: From diapers to drugs: How India's global corporate hubs are putting AI to work
content.summary: Whether they are recruiting the ideal "momfluencer" to sell diapers or trawling through reams of data to fast-track drug launches, global centers of multinational ‌firms in India are increasingly putting AI to work in creative ways. Heads of several Global ‌Capability Centres (GCCs) told Reuters they are deploying AI across a host of functions - from marketing and content creation to finance and human resources - ​to automate time-consuming, repetitive tasks that once required hours of manual effort. And the ambition doesn't stop there.

headline: Marvell Stock Drops After Earnings Beat. It Sees Growth in Powering the AI Boom.
summary: The data-center boom is still going strong for  Marvell Technology  The chip company reported better-than-expected first-quarter revenue and sees higher revenue than expected in the current quarter.  Marvell stock dropped 2.6% in Thursday’s premarket following the results.  The company reported revenue of $2.42 billion, compared with Wall Street estimates of $2.41 billion, according to FactSet.

content.title: Marvell Stock Drops After Earnings Beat. It Sees Growth in Powering the AI Boom.
content.summary: The data-center boom is still going strong for  Marvell Technology  The chip company reported better-than-expected first-quarter revenue and sees higher revenue than expected in the current quarter.  Marvell stock dropped 2.6% in Thursday’s premarket following the results.  The company reported revenue of $2.42 billion, compared with Wall Street estimates of $2.41 billion, according to FactSet.
